apiVersion: v3 (docs site versioned as v3.0.1; REST paths under /api/v3)

versioningPolicy.current: All REST endpoints served under /api/v3. Docs site exposes a version selector (v3.0.1) and a public changelog at https://docs.coingecko.com/changelog.
versioningPolicy.deprecationPolicy: not documented (no formal versioning/deprecation policy page found on the official docs site; changes are announced via the changelog)

authentication.recommended: Header method. Docs: 'Store your API key in your backend and use a proxy to inject it into requests.' and 'Avoid query string parameters in production — they risk exposing your key in logs and browser history.'
authentication.demoApiDifference: Demo API uses a different key parameter (docs error code 10002: 'Pro API requires x_cg_pro_api_key, Demo API requires x_cg_demo_api_key') and a different root URL (error codes 10010/10011 fire on invalid key-type/root-URL combinations).

baseUrls.use: On-chain DEX (GeckoTerminal) endpoints — same auth, '/onchain' appended to path

endpoints.name: Simple Price
endpoints.pagination: none (max 515 ids per request; include_tokens=all limited to 50 symbols per request)
endpoints.freshness: cached/near-real-time: updated every 20 seconds (paid); every 60 seconds (Demo/keyless)

endpoints.name: Simple Token Price by Contract Addresses
requiredParams: id (path — asset platform id, refers to /asset_platforms, e.g. 'ethereum')
responseFields: object keyed by contract address: {vs_currency} price
endpoints.pagination: none (maximum of 515 contract addresses per request)
endpoints.freshness: cached every 20 seconds (paid); every 60 seconds (Demo/keyless)

endpoints.name: Asset Platforms List
endpoints.freshness: cached every 60 seconds (paid); every 5 minutes (Demo/keyless)

endpoints.name: Coins List with Market Data
endpoints.freshness: cached/near-real-time: every 30 seconds (paid); every 60 seconds (Demo/keyless). Filter priority when combined: category > ids > names > symbols; wildcard searches not supported

endpoints.name: Coin Historical Chart Data
endpoints.freshness: cached every 30 seconds; last completed daily point available 10 minutes after midnight (00:10 UTC). Auto-granularity: 1 day = 5-minutely; 2-90 days = hourly; >90 days = daily (00:00 UTC). interval=hourly limited to past 100 days; interval=5m past 10 days and Enterprise only

endpoints.name: Coin Historical Chart Data within Time Range
endpoints.pagination: none (bounded by from/to; hourly max any 100 days per request; 5m max any 10 days per request, Enterprise only)
endpoints.freshness: cache: 30 seconds (1-day ranges), 30 minutes (2-90 day ranges), 12 hours (>90 day ranges). Historical availability: hourly from 30 Jan 2018; 5m from 9 Feb 2018; daily unlimited range

endpoints.name: Coin Historical Data by Date (snapshot)
endpoints.freshness: EOD snapshot at 00:00:00 UTC for the given date; last completed UTC day available 35 minutes after midnight (00:35 UTC); cache every 30 minutes

endpoints.name: Coin OHLC Chart
optionalParams: interval ('daily'|'hourly' — paid plans only; empty = auto)
responseFields: array of [timestamp, open, high, low, close]
endpoints.freshness: cache every 15 minutes. Auto-granularity candle body: 1-2 days = 30 min; 3-30 days = 4 hours; 31+ days = 4 days. OHLC within arbitrary time range requires /coins/{id}/ohlc/range (Analyst+)

endpoints.name: Coin Tickers (CEX + DEX)
endpoints.pagination: page parameter; 'Tickers are paginated to 100 items per page.'
endpoints.freshness: cached every 60 seconds

endpoints.name: Coins List (ID map)
optionalParams: not verified in detail (listed on endpoint overview as the canonical id/name/symbol map)
endpoints.freshness: not verified for this endpoint

endpoints.name: Coin Categories with Market Data
endpoints.freshness: cached every 5 minutes

endpoints.name: Top Gainers & Losers
endpoints.freshness: not verified

endpoints.name: Exchanges with Data
endpoints.freshness: cached every 60 seconds. 'Only exchanges with active trading volume on CoinGecko are included. Inactive or deactivated exchanges are removed from the list.'

endpoints.name: Derivatives Tickers
endpoints.freshness: cached every 30 seconds; 'all the tickers from derivatives exchanges on CoinGecko'

endpoints.name: Global Crypto Market Data
endpoints.freshness: cached every 10 minutes

endpoints.name: On-chain Networks List (GeckoTerminal)
endpoints.freshness: cached every 60 seconds

endpoints.name: On-chain Top Pools by Token Address (GeckoTerminal)
optionalParams: page (default 1; pagination beyond 10 pages requires Analyst plan or above)
responseFields: data[].attributes: address, name, pool_created_at, base_token_price_usd, quote_token_price_usd, base_token_price_native_currency, quote_token_price_native_currency, base_token_price_quote_token, quote_token_price_base_token, token_price_usd, fdv_usd, market_cap_usd, price_change_percentage (m5/m15/m30/h1/h6/h24), transactions (buys/sells/buyers/sellers), volume_usd (m5..h24), reserve_in_usd — numeric values are JSON strings
data[].relationships: base_token/quote_token (data.id like 'eth_0x…', type 'token'), dex (data.id like 'uniswap-v4-ethereum'), network
included[] (when include= used): token {address, name, symbol, decimals, image_url, coingecko_coin_id}, dex {name}
endpoints.pagination: page parameter; 20 pools per page; beyond 10 pages requires Analyst plan or above
endpoints.freshness: cached every 30 seconds (paid); every 60 seconds (Demo/keyless)

endpoints.name: On-chain Pool OHLCV (GeckoTerminal)
responseFields: ohlcv_list as [timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume]
endpoints.pagination: cursor-style via before_timestamp + limit; max 6-month range per call
endpoints.freshness: cached every 10 seconds (paid); every 60 seconds (Demo/keyless). Historical: 'Analyst plan and above can access data from September 2021 to present'

endpoints.name: On-chain Pool Trades (GeckoTerminal)
responseFields: last 300 trades of a pool (per endpoint overview)
endpoints.freshness: not verified for REST; WebSocket OnchainTrade channel documented as 'as fast as 0.1s for actively traded pools' (Analyst+ beta)

endpoints.name: API Usage / Key Status
endpoints.freshness: not documented
endpoints.planRestriction: Available to 'Basic, Analyst, Lite, Pro, and Enterprise' plan subscribers

assetClasses: crypto derivatives (perpetuals and futures tickers: price, funding rate, open interest, basis, spread)
coverage.traditionalAssetClasses: none — no equities, FX, fixed income, or commodities datasets are documented
coverage.coins: 18,000+ coins (docs homepage figure per search results); marketing page claims '41M+' tokens tracked across networks (includes on-chain tokens)
coverage.exchanges: 1,500+ exchanges; 'more than 36,000,000 trading pairs on CEXs and DEXs' (marketing page). Only exchanges with active trading volume are included in /exchanges
coverage.chains: 200+ blockchain networks and 1,000+ DEXes via GeckoTerminal on-chain endpoints (marketing page); docs homepage cites '200+ blockchain networks, 1,800+ DEXes, 39M+ tokens' for onchain DEX data
coverage.nfts: NFT collections listed on CoinGecko; docs search results cite 3,000+ NFT collections
coverage.geographies: global aggregation across exchanges; exchange objects carry a country field; no geography-specific market entitlements documented

freshness.model: REST responses are cached aggregates, not exchange-direct real-time feeds. Update cadence varies per endpoint and plan.
freshness.marketChart: every 30 s; daily point finalized 00:10 UTC
freshness.marketChartRange: 30 s (1-day range) / 30 min (2-90 days) / 12 h (>90 days)
freshness.historySnapshot: EOD snapshot at 00:00 UTC, available 00:35 UTC; cache 30 min
freshness.websocket: push streaming (Beta, Analyst+): OnchainSimpleTokenPrice 'as fast as 1s'; OnchainTrade 'as fast as 0.1s'; OnchainOHLCV intervals 1s/1m/5m/15m/1h/2h/4h/8h/12h/1d; CGSimplePrice for coin prices

historicalDepth.dailyMarketData: Marketing page: '12+ years of historical data' 'starting from 2014'. Pricing page gates depth by plan: Demo/Basic '2 years historical data'; Analyst/Lite/Enterprise '10 years historical data'. (Apparent tension between '12+ years' marketing claim and '10 years' plan entitlement is unresolved in the docs.)
historicalDepth.hourlyMarketData: available 'from 30 Jan 2018'; max 'any 100 days' per request
historicalDepth.fiveMinuteMarketData: available 'from 9 Feb 2018'; max 'any 10 days' per request; Enterprise only
historicalDepth.onchainOhlcv: 'Analyst plan and above can access data from September 2021 to present' with 'max 6-month range' per call
historicalDepth.eodSnapshots: /coins/{id}/history returns 00:00 UTC snapshots per date (depth subject to plan historical-data entitlement)

pagination.primaryMechanism: page + per_page query parameters on list endpoints (per_page typically 1-250, default 100)
pagination.tickers: page parameter, fixed 100 items per page

platformMappings.note: Platform Chain enum -> CoinGecko asset platform id (for /simple/token_price/{id}) and -> GeckoTerminal onchain network id (for /onchain/networks/{network}/...). Verified against the official GeckoTerminal /networks response (network id + coingecko_asset_platform_id pairs), the official /asset_platforms response, and official docs examples. Chains not listed here are unmapped — adapters must refuse them.

rateLimits.model: Per-minute rate limit plus monthly call-credit quota. 'Each successful request (HTTP 200) deducts 1 credit from your monthly quota, while failed requests (4xx, 5xx) do not consume credits but still count toward your per-minute rate limit.' WebSocket: '0.1 credit per response returned.'
rateLimits.monitoring: GET /key returns plan, rate_limit_request_per_minute, monthly_call_credit, current_remaining_monthly_calls
rateLimits.backoffGuidance: REST: none documented beyond 429 message 'Rate limit exceeded. Reduce call frequency or upgrade your plan'. No Retry-After header documented. WebSocket: 'Clients should implement automatic reconnection with exponential backoff.'

httpStatusCodes.408: Request took too long — usually caused by slow network on your end
httpStatusCodes.429: Rate limit exceeded. Reduce call frequency or upgrade your plan

vendorErrorCodes.10002: No API key provided. Pro API requires x_cg_pro_api_key, Demo API requires x_cg_demo_api_key
vendorErrorCodes.10005: Endpoint not available on your plan
vendorErrorCodes.10010/10011: Invalid key type used with incorrect root URL

errors.corsNote: CORS errors: 'Server didn't return CORS headers. Proxy requests through your backend instead'
errors.responseBodySchema: not documented — the errors page lists codes and messages but no formal JSON error-body schema
errors.creditImpact: Failed requests (4xx/5xx) do not consume monthly credits but do count toward the per-minute rate limit

enterpriseExclusive: dedicated CSM, integration consultation, Slack support
paidPlanFeatures: faster cache cadence (e.g. 20s simple/price, 30s coins/markets, 10s onchain OHLCV)
entitlements.websocket: 'CoinGecko WebSocket (Beta) is available for paid plan customers (Analyst plan & above)'; self-serve limits: 10 concurrent connections, 100 subscriptions per channel per socket; Enterprise gets higher limits; excluded from standard SLA while in beta

sdks.status: 'Maintained by the CoinGecko team, compatible with both Pro and Demo APIs'; docs reference section labels the SDK 'CoinGecko SDK (Beta)'
'Maintained by the CoinGecko team, compatible with both Pro and Demo APIs'; docs reference section labels the SDK 'CoinGecko SDK (Beta)'

licensing.licenseGrant: Limited, non-exclusive, non-assignable, non-transferable license to use the API to develop/test/support applications and integrate the API with your Application (API Terms of Service).
licensing.redistribution: TOS s4.1.6: 'You are not permitted to sell, rent, lease, sub-license, re-distribute or syndicate access to the CoinGecko API' except under an Executed Agreement. Pricing page: Enterprise custom license covers 'redistribution of data and white labeling'.
licensing.storageCaching: TOS s6.1 permits limited caching with conditions including 'You should refresh the cache at least every 24 hours' and strong encryption/security measures. TOS s6.2: 'you are not allowed to duplicate, reproduce, copy, store, derive from or translate any Data' (outside the permitted caching scope) — long-term historical storage or derived-data products likely require an Enterprise/custom agreement.
licensing.attribution: Commercial plans must 'prominently display the message: Data provided by CoinGecko' with a hyperlink to https://www.coingecko.com/en/api (pricing page). TOS s4.4 requires displaying 'Powered by CoinGecko' 'in a legible font... no smaller than font size 10' and compliance with CoinGecko Brand Guidelines (brand.coingecko.com).
licensing.termination: TOS s10.4.3: on termination 'you undertake to promptly and permanently delete all Data and other information procured from... the CoinGecko API', with written certification if requested.
licensing.abuseClauses: TOS s4.2: usage exceeding 'reasonable request volume' or constituting 'excessive or abusive usage' permits immediate termination.

limitations: Aggregated, cached data — not exchange-direct feeds. REST cache cadence ranges from 10-20 seconds (best endpoints, paid) to 12 hours (long-range chart queries); must not be presented as tick-level real-time.
No traditional asset classes: crypto/NFT/DEX only — cannot serve equities, FX, fixed income, or commodities functions.
5-minutely historical granularity is Enterprise-only; hourly data exists only from 30 Jan 2018 and is capped at 100 days per request; 5m from 9 Feb 2018 capped at 10 days per request.
Historical depth is plan-gated: 2 years (Demo/Basic) vs 10 years (Analyst+) per the pricing page, while marketing claims '12+ years since 2014' — the exact interaction is not clarified in docs.

Symbol mapping: the API is keyed on CoinGecko-specific coin IDs (via /coins/list); symbols are not unique (symbols filter limited to 50 with include_tokens=all; 'Wildcard searches are not supported').
Ticker quality varies by venue: responses carry is_anomaly and is_stale flags and a nullable trust_score — consumers must filter these for institutional use.
Survivorship consideration: 'Inactive or deactivated exchanges are removed from the list' on /exchanges, so current listings do not reflect delisted/defunct venues.
Daily historical points finalize at 00:00 UTC and become available 10-35 minutes after midnight depending on endpoint (00:10 for market_chart, 00:35 for /history).
Failed requests (4xx/5xx) still count toward the per-minute rate limit.
No documented REST backoff guidance or Retry-After semantics; error response body schema not formally documented.
WebSocket is Beta, Analyst+ only, 'excluded from standard SLA protections', with 10-connection / 100-subscription self-serve limits.
On-chain DEX OHLCV history begins September 2021, max 6-month window per call, Analyst+ for historical access.

TOS restricts storage/derivation of Data (s6.1/6.2, 24-hour cache refresh) and requires deletion on termination — a compliance constraint for research platforms that persist historical data without an Enterprise agreement.

Plan naming inconsistency in docs: /key page and WebSocket page reference 'Pro' and 'Pro+' plans that do not appear on the current pricing page (Demo/Basic/Analyst/Lite/Enterprise) — likely legacy tiers.
OHLC endpoint auto-granularity produces coarse candles (4-day candles beyond 31 days) unless paid interval parameters are used.

primary: Crypto spot reference/market data: prices, market caps, volumes, supply, ATH/ATL across 18,000+ coins (coins/markets, simple/price)
Crypto historical research series: daily since 2014 (plan-gated depth), hourly since 2018, EOD UTC snapshots (/coins/{id}/history)
Crypto sector/category analytics (/coins/categories) and global market aggregates (/global)
Exchange metadata and venue quality screening (trust_score, 2%-depth cost metrics via tickers?depth=true)
Crypto derivatives overview: funding rates, open interest, basis, spread across derivatives exchanges (/derivatives)
NFT collection market data (Analyst+ for market lists/charts)
On-chain DEX analytics via GeckoTerminal: 200+ networks, pools, token OHLCV since Sep 2021, trades, holders (mostly Analyst+)
Public treasury holdings of companies/governments (entities/public_treasury endpoints)
fallback: Near-real-time crypto price display where 20-60 s cached aggregates are acceptable and clearly labeled (WebSocket Beta can improve latency for Analyst+ but is SLA-excluded)
DEX trade-level data (REST last-300-trades window; WebSocket OnchainTrade for streaming)
notSuitable: Any non-crypto asset class (equities, FX, rates, commodities) — no coverage
Tick-level or exchange-direct market data for execution/surveillance use
Full order-book data (only 2% depth cost metrics documented)
Redistribution/white-label data products without an Enterprise custom license
hardConstraints: Cached/delayed data must never be presented as real-time; per-endpoint cache cadence must be surfaced in the platform (20 s to 12 h depending on endpoint/range)
Attribution 'Data provided by CoinGecko' + hyperlink required on commercial plans; brand guidelines apply
No resale/redistribution/syndication without an Executed (Enterprise) Agreement; TOS caching limited to 24-hour refresh cycles and prohibits deriving/storing Data beyond that scope
5-minutely intraday history and supply charts require Enterprise; many research endpoints require Analyst+
All Data must be deleted (with written certification on request) upon contract termination
Failed calls count toward rate limits — client must implement its own throttling/backoff since none is documented for REST

sourcesConsulted.whatItVerified: Docs root: Pro vs Demo API structure, onchain DEX coverage claim (200+ networks, 1,800+ DEXes, 39M+ tokens), delivery methods (REST, WebSocket, Webhooks), docs navigation
Pro API auth: x-cg-pro-api-key header, x_cg_pro_api_key query param, base URL https://pro-api.coingecko.com/api/v3/, /onchain path convention, key-handling guidance
HTTP status codes (400/401/403/408/429/500/503/1020), vendor error codes (10002/10005/10010/10011), CORS note, credit vs rate-limit accounting, Demo 100 calls/min
Complete endpoint catalog with exact paths and Analyst+ / Enterprise exclusivity markers, including onchain (GeckoTerminal) endpoints

Plan tiers, prices, monthly credits, per-minute rate limits, endpoint counts per tier, 2y vs 10y historical depth, REST/WebSocket/Webhook access per tier, commercial attribution requirement, Enterprise custom licensing and SLA
/coins/markets method, params, defaults, per_page limits, response fields, pagination, 30s/60s cache cadence, filter priority
market_chart params, auto-granularity rules, interval plan restrictions (5m Enterprise), response arrays, 30s cache, 00:10 UTC daily availability
range params, granularity limits (hourly 100 days, 5m 10 days Enterprise), historical availability dates (hourly 30 Jan 2018, 5m 9 Feb 2018), tiered cache cadence
/simple/price params, 515-id limit, response fields, 20s/60s cache cadence
/coins/{id}/history params, 00:00 UTC snapshot semantics, 00:35 UTC availability, response fields, 30-min cache
/coins/{id}/ohlc params, allowed days values, auto-granularity candle bodies, paid-plan interval access, [ts,o,h,l,c] format, 15-min cache
tickers params (depth, order, dex_pair_format), 100-per-page pagination, is_anomaly/is_stale/trust_score fields, 60s cache
/derivatives response fields (funding_rate, open_interest, basis, contract_type), 30s cache
/exchanges params, response fields (trust_score, trade_volume_24h_btc), active-exchanges-only inclusion rule, 60s cache
/global response schema and 10-minute cache
/coins/categories params, response fields, 5-minute cache
onchain pool OHLCV path, timeframes, aggregate/before_timestamp/limit params, Sep-2021 history start, 6-month range cap, 10s/60s cache, Analyst+ restriction
GET /key response fields for quota monitoring and plan availability list (Basic, Analyst, Lite, Pro, Enterprise)
Official TypeScript and Python SDKs, 'Maintained by the CoinGecko team, compatible with both Pro and Demo APIs'
TOS clauses: caching (s6.1, 24h refresh), derivation/storage prohibition (s6.2), redistribution prohibition (s4.1.6), attribution (s4.4), deletion on termination (s10.4.3), abuse clauses (s4.2)
Marketing coverage claims: 41M+ tokens, 1,500+ exchanges, 36M+ pairs, 200+ networks, 1,000+ DEXes, 12+ years history since 2014, 99.9% uptime, named clients

WebSocket Beta availability (Analyst+), channels (CGSimplePrice, OnchainSimpleTokenPrice, OnchainTrade, OnchainOHLCV), connection/subscription limits, 0.1 credit per response, exponential-backoff reconnection guidance, SLA exclusion
Existence of public changelog (via search results); no formal deprecation policy page located

GET /simple/token_price/{id}: path/params (contract_addresses, vs_currencies, include_* flags, precision), response keyed by contract address, 515-address cap, 20s/60s cache cadence, example asset platform id 'ethereum'

GET /onchain/networks/{network}/tokens/{token_address}/pools: params (include, include_inactive_source, page, sort, include_gt_community_data), 20 pools/page, >10 pages Analyst+, attribute fields carry numeric values as JSON strings, relationships token ids formatted '{network}_{address}', included token attributes (address, name, symbol, decimals, image_url, coingecko_coin_id) and dex attributes (name), 30s/60s cache, example network id 'eth'
GET /asset_platforms: params (filter=nft), response fields (id, chain_identifier, name, shortname, native_coin_id, image), example ids 'solana' and 'flow-evm', 60s/5min cache
GET /onchain/networks: response fields (data[].id, attributes.name, attributes.coingecko_asset_platform_id), example pairs eth->ethereum and bsc->binance-smart-chain, 60s cache

Official GeckoTerminal networks list (live, keyless): confirmed network ids eth/solana/polygon_pos/arbitrum/optimism/base/bsc/avax with coingecko_asset_platform_id values ethereum/solana/polygon-pos/arbitrum-one/optimistic-ethereum/base/binance-smart-chain/avalanche
Official asset platforms list (live, keyless): cross-checked 'polygon-pos' asset platform id

openQuestions: No formal API versioning/deprecation policy document was found — how much notice does CoinGecko give before breaking changes? (Changelog exists but policy is not stated.)
Exact Enterprise rate limits, credits, and pricing are 'custom/inquire' — must be obtained from sales.
How the pricing page's '2 years / 10 years historical data' entitlement interacts with the '12+ years since 2014' marketing claim and per-endpoint availability dates is not documented precisely.
Error response body JSON schema is not formally documented (only codes/messages).
WebSocket endpoint URL and its authentication handshake were not captured from the docs excerpt reviewed.
Docs reference legacy plan names ('Pro', 'Pro+') on /key and WebSocket pages that are absent from the current pricing page — current mapping unconfirmed.
Webhooks capability is listed for Analyst+ but its documentation was not reviewed in this pass.
Whether TOS s6.1/6.2 storage limits (24h cache refresh, no derived storage) can be waived below Enterprise tier for internal research persistence requires legal/sales confirmation.

Verifier finding 2026-07-17: /coins/{id}/ohlc documents interval=daily only for days 1/7/14/30/90/180 and interval=hourly only for days 1/7/14/30 (and 90); other days/interval combinations are undocumented — adapter now restricts accordingly.
